pi_items.source: Task instructions and setup_task.sh which clears this specific path before the task begins.

Python geometric intersection script run against the ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp. The shapefile has exactly 10 countries crossing the equator (Lat 0), 9 of which are explicitly required in this list.
Python geometric intersection script run against the ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp. None of these countries' polygons intersect the equator line.
Python geometric intersection script run against the ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp reveals exactly 10 matching country polygons.
Logical upper bound for an imprecise manual selection. Given the exact count is 10, a max of 16 allows a small margin of error (e.g., mistakenly selecting bordering countries).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the public Natural Earth 1:110m scale country boundary dataset (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp). Geometric analysis of the dataset confirms that exactly 10 countries intersect the equator (Latitude 0°): Indonesia (IDN), Dem. Rep. Congo (COD), Somalia (SOM), Kenya (KEN), Brazil (BRA), Colombia (COL), Ecuador (ECU), Congo (COG), Gabon (GAB), and Uganda (UGA). The task correctly identifies 9 of these 10 for mandatory inclusion and expects a minimum feature count of 10. The excluded countries list contains genuine non-equatorial countries.
